hero.subtitle: If we classify and recycle waste correctly today, we help protect the environment and conserve resources for the future.

bins.label: Learn to sort
bins.intro: The first step of recycling is knowing where everything goes. Colored bins are a universal guide: one glance at the color and you know what goes inside.
bins.imageAlt: Recycling bins: paper, glass, plastic, e-waste, metal and organic

how.label: How it works
how.intro: Behind the simplicity there is a convolutional neural network fine-tuned on the RealWaste dataset from the UCI ML Repository.
how.step1Desc: Take a photo of the waste in good light or upload it from your gallery. Works with JPG, JPEG and PNG.
how.step2Desc: ResNet50 with Transfer Learning extracts 2048 features from your photo and runs them through a fine-tuned head trained on RealWaste.
how.step3Desc: Predicted category, probability distribution per class and the correct recycling recommendation.

categories.label: Supported categories
categories.intro: Each prediction comes with the right bin and a practical recommendation. Drag the carousel to explore.

cardboard.name: Cardboard
cardboard.description: Boxes, packaging and clean cardboard sheets.
cardboard.tip: Flatten the boxes to save bin space.

organic.name: Organic
organic.description: Food scraps, peels and compostable waste.
organic.tip: No plastics or wrappers: straight to compost.

glass.name: Glass
glass.description: Bottles, jars and rinsed glass containers.
glass.tip: No lids or corks; they go through a different stream.

metal.name: Metal
metal.description: Cans, aluminum and small metal pieces.
metal.tip: Crush cans to reduce their volume.

misc.name: Miscellaneous
misc.description: Waste that doesn't fit other categories.
misc.tip: When in doubt, don't contaminate valid recyclables.

paper.name: Paper
paper.tip: No grease, no stuck food, no plastics.

plastic.name: Plastic
plastic.description: Containers, bottles and recyclable plastic film.
plastic.tip: Check the recycling code on the bottom.

textile.name: Textile
textile.description: Clothes, sheets and fabrics in general.
textile.tip: If still in good shape, consider donating it.

vegetation.name: Vegetation
vegetation.description: Leaves, branches and green garden waste.

items.title: Source separation
items.copy: The first step for waste to re-enter the productive cycle. What gets mixed gets contaminated.

items.title: Accessible AI
items.copy: One photo is enough. No complex forms: a tool ready to use from your phone.

items.title: Open education
items.copy: Academic project with public code and data. Designed to learn and replicate.

cta.intro: Upload an image, get the category, learn the right bin. A tool that fits in any pocket.

classify.metaDescription: Take a photo with the camera or upload an image and a ResNet50 model tells you which bin your waste belongs in. 9 categories, prediction in <1s.
classify.label: Classify your waste
classify.intro: Take a photo with your phone camera or upload an image (JPG, JPEG or PNG). The model tells you which of the 9 categories it belongs to and which bin it should go in.
classify.uploadTip: Ideally with a plain background and good light.
classify.noteBody1: This interface needs the classification API to be running. If the API is off or doesn't respond, you will see an error message in the result panel. The default URL is
classify.noteBody2: (local FastAPI). If you have it deployed elsewhere, use the
classify.openExplorer: or click to open the file picker
classify.fileFormats: JPG · JPEG · PNG · plain background recommended
classify.emptyMessageTail: to see the predicted category and probability distribution here.
classify.errorTitleDefault: Could not connect to the API
classify.errorTitleMixed: Mixed content blocked by the browser
classify.errorMixedDetail: You are on an HTTPS page calling an HTTP API. Change the URL to https:// (or run the web locally at http://localhost:3000).
classify.errorNetworkDetailA: Check that the API is online at
classify.errorUnknown: Unknown error while querying the API.
classify.camTitle: Take a photo of the waste
classify.camHint: Center the object, with good light and a plain background.
classify.camPermission: Accept the permission in the browser.
classify.camLiveReady: Camera live, ready to take the photo.
classify.camNotReady: The camera is still focusing. Wait a second and take the photo again.
classify.camErrUnsupported: Your browser doesn't allow using the camera from the web, or the page isn't served over HTTPS. Try uploading an image or using the system camera.
classify.camErrDenied: You denied camera permission. Enable it in your browser’s site settings, or tap “System camera” to take the photo without web permission.
classify.camErrNotfound: We couldn't find any camera available on this device.
classify.camErrInuse: The camera is being used by another app. Close it and try again.
classify.camErrUnknown: We couldn't open the camera. Try again or upload an image.

about.metaDescription: Meet the team and tech behind EcoClasificador: ResNet50 + RealWaste, an educational tool to recycle better.
about.introBody: We make correct source separation of waste easier with an accessible tool: one photo is enough to know which bin it belongs in.
about.missionTitle: Reduce the impact, one photo at a time.
about.missionBody: Our mission is to lower the friction of doing things right. No course or heavy app required: a browser, a photo, a clear answer.

blocks.title: The first step
blocks.copy: Correct source separation is the first step so waste can return to the productive cycle instead of ending up in a landfill.

blocks.title: Useful technology
blocks.copy: Reducing urban waste impact by offering a visual, automatic guide accessible from any device.

blocks.title: Open learning
blocks.copy: Open code and data. Designed so students, teachers and municipalities can replicate and improve it.

about.techBody: The engine is a ResNet50 convolutional neural network fine-tuned on RealWaste (UCI). The REST API receives the image and returns the class + probability distribution.
about.techReferenceBody: Single, S., Iranmanesh, S., & Raad, R. (2023). RealWaste [Dataset]. UCI Machine Learning Repository.
about.ethicsBody1Part1: EcoClasificador is an educational, experimental project. Its predictions have a margin of error and
about.ethicsBody1Strong: do not replace local recycling regulations
about.ethicsBody1Part2: nor the decision of a professional operator.
about.ethicsBody2: For critical decisions — hazardous waste, WEEE, special materials — always consult the current regulations in your municipality.

footer.tagline: Artificial intelligence in service of recycling. Sort today, preserve tomorrow.

notFound.title: Nothing to recycle here.
notFound.body: The URL you're looking for doesn't exist or has moved. Go back home or try the classifier.

config.title: Site settings
config.intro: Adjust the institutional colors, typography and favicon. Changes apply live and are saved in your browser.
config.colorsHint: Pick a predefined palette or switch to custom mode to edit each token.
config.fontHint: The font family applies to headings and body text.
config.faviconHint: PNG or SVG, up to 1 MB. Recommended 512×512 px.
config.customEditingMode: Editing palette for {mode} mode. Toggle the theme to edit the other mode.

tokenHints.olive: Olive green, used on accents and borders.

favicon.hint: We accept PNG and SVG. Max 1 MB. Recommended 512×512.

errors.type: Unsupported format. Use PNG or SVG.

warnings.dimensions: Dimensions are not 512×512. Favicon may look pixelated.

preview.cardBody: This is how a card looks with your new palette.

actions.resetConfirm: Are you sure? Your custom colors, typography and favicon will be erased.
